title: Say something about yourself
checkLabels.t1: The heading must still be there
checkLabels.t4: The sentence must be INSIDE the paragraph
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.lost-heading: The heading is gone. The task is to ADD a sentence below it, not to replace it. Put <h1> back.
why.no-p:
The sentence is there, but it is bare text. The browser does not know it is a sentence — to it, these are just letters. Wrap it in

.
why.no-close:
You opened

but never closed it.

is missing.
why.empty-p: The paragraph is there, but it is empty. Your sentence sits outside it.
hint2.empty-p:
Look at where

ends and where your sentence sits. One is after the other, but it should be inside.
hint3.empty-p:
Wrapping means: tag in front, text in the middle, tag behind.

HERE

, not

HERE.
blocks.0.text: The heading is done. Now you want a sentence below it — for example, what you do.
blocks.2.text: Just write the sentence after . Press Enter, go to a new line, write it. Run.
blocks.3.text: It works, right? It appears. But it is bare text — hanging there, without being anything in particular. The browser does not know it is a sentence. To it, this is just... letters.
blocks.6.text: The letter "p" comes from "paragraph". You wrap the sentence and it is no longer random text, but a paragraph.
blocks.7.text:
You will say: it looks almost the same. Yes. But try writing two sentences one below the other WITHOUT

— you will see they glue into one line, no matter how many times you press Enter. With

each stands on its own.
blocks.8.text: The first lesson of the web: the blank lines you see in your code do not exist for the browser.
